name: French Way and Ucieza River Loop from Villalcázar de Sirga
name_original: Orilla del río Ucieza – circular desde Villalcázar de Sirga (EV3)
description: This easy 12.6-mile touring cycling route along the Ucieza River from Villalcázar de Sirga offers historical sights and riparian views.
region_intro: Touring cycling around Villalcázar de Sirga offers routes across the flat landscape of the Tierra de Campos region, making it suitable for long-distance cycling. The area features open vistas of the Castilian plain and is traversed by the historic Camino de Santiago. The Ucieza river flows nearby, adding subtle natural elements to the terrain. Best touring cycling routes around Villalcázar De Sirga The most popular touring cycling route is Vía Aquitania – circular desde Carrión de los Condes (EV3), a 26.9 miles (43.2 km) trail that takes 3 hours 22 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers expansive views across the Castilian plain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is French Way and Ucieza River Loop from Villalcázar de Sirga, an easy 12.6 miles (20.3 km) path. This route combines sections of the Camino de Santiago with views of the Ucieza river. Local touring cyclists also love the Robladillo de Ucieza, Villasarracino y Villalcázar de Sigra – circular desde Carrión de los Condes, a 24.7 miles (39.8 km) trail leading through rural landscapes, often completed in about 3 hours 28 minutes. Touring cycling around Villalcázar de Sirga is defined by flat terrain, open Castilian plains, and sections of the Camino de Santiago. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy loops to more challenging long-distance routes. The routes in Villalcázar de Sirga are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. More than 150 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Villalcázar de Sirga's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Villalcázar de Sirga?
A: There are 5 touring cycling routes around Villalcázar de Sirga, offering a variety of experiences across the flat landscape of the Tierra de Campos region. Over 150 touring cyclists have already explored these routes using komoot.

Q: What is the general terrain like for touring cycling in Villalcázar de Sirga?
A: The terrain around Villalcázar de Sirga is predominantly flat, characteristic of the Tierra de Campos region. This makes it highly suitable for touring cycling, allowing for long distances with relative ease and offering open vistas of the Castilian plain. Some routes also feature views of the Ucieza river.

Q: Are there touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Villalcázar de Sirga?
A: Yes, there is at least one easy touring cycling route perfect for beginners or families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175182934/french-way-and-ucieza-river-loop-from-villalcazar-de-sirga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;French Way and Ucieza River Loop from Villalcázar de Sirga&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 12.6-mile (20.3 km) path that combines sections of the Camino de Santiago with river views.

Q: Are there any challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Yes, for experienced cyclists looking for a longer challenge, there is one difficult route available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40298185" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Zoilo – Frómista loop from San Román de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt; is a demanding 64.6-mile (103.9 km) route that offers a significant distance and elevation gain.

Q: Can I combine touring cycling with exploring historical sites in Villalcázar de Sirga?
A: Absolutely. Villalcázar de Sirga is strategically located on the historic Camino de Santiago, and many routes integrate this cultural heritage. The town itself boasts the monumental 13th-century Church of Santa María la Blanca, founded by the Templars, which is a significant historical and artistic landmark. Nearby towns like Frómista also offer Romanesque architecture.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes around Villalcázar de Sirga?
A: Yes, several routes are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175143288/via-aquitania-circular-desde-carrion-de-los-condes-ev3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Aquitania – circular desde Carrión de los Condes (EV3)&lt;/a&gt;, a 26.9-mile (43.2 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175529457/robladillo-de-ucieza-villasarracino-y-villalcazar-de-sigra-circular-desde-carrion-de-los-condes" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robladillo de Ucieza, Villasarracino y Villalcázar de Sigra – circular desde Carrión de los Condes&lt;/a&gt;, which is 24.7 miles (39.8 km).

Q: What kind of surfaces can I expect on the touring cycling routes in this region?
A: The routes in the Villalcázar de Sirga area, especially those along the Camino de Santiago, feature a mix of surfaces. You can expect sections of compacted dirt tracks, rural trails, and low-traffic secondary roads. Approximately 40% of the primary Camino path is paved, making it suitable for various touring bikes.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while cycling near Villalcázar de Sirga?
A: While cycling, you can enjoy open vistas of the Castilian plain. For specific landmarks, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4930123" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Zoilo&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632666" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Santa María del Camino Church&lt;/a&gt;. The nearby town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4541372" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Frómista&lt;/a&gt; is also known for its Romanesque churches.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Villalcázar de Sirga?
A: The touring cycling routes around Villalcázar de Sirga are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 10 reviews. Cyclists often praise the accessible, flat terrain and the opportunity to experience the historic Camino de Santiago.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that pass through other significant Camino towns?
A: Yes, Villalcázar de Sirga is conveniently located between other important Camino stops like Frómista and Carrión de los Condes. Routes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175143288/via-aquitania-circular-desde-carrion-de-los-condes-ev3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Vía Aquitania – circular desde Carrión de los Condes (EV3)&lt;/a&gt; start from Carrión de los Condes, allowing you to explore the broader Romanesque heritage of Palencia.

Q: Is there a touring cycling route that offers views of the Ucieza river?
A: Yes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1175182934/french-way-and-ucieza-river-loop-from-villalcazar-de-sirga" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;French Way and Ucieza River Loop from Villalcázar de Sirga&lt;/a&gt; specifically combines sections of the Camino de Santiago with scenic views of the Ucieza river, providing a pleasant natural element to your ride.
tips:
It almost looks like a cathedral! Its size, the portico and the rose window that floods the chapel of Santiago with light in the interior stand out.
It can be visited every day and with the pilgrim's credential it costs one euro per person. Children enter free.
The longer the more you see the pilgrims on the way.
Pleasant path near the Ucieza river between Villalcázar de Sirga and Revenga de Campos.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route is explicitly rated as easy for touring cycling. It features mostly flat or gently undulating paths, making it suitable for a wide range of fitness levels, including beginners and families looking for a relaxed experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Riverbank of the Ucieza cycling route?
faqs.answer: Given its easy difficulty, the terrain is likely to feature mostly flat or gently undulating paths. You can expect compacted earth, gravel, or paved surfaces, typical of easy cycling routes in Spain, especially those along riverbanks.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Riverbank of the Ucieza circular route?
faqs.answer: The route offers a serene cycling experience along the Ucieza River, with views of riparian vegetation and agricultural fields. You'll also pass by the historic town of Villalcázar de Sirga, where you can visit the monumental 13th-century Church of Santa María la Blanca. Other notable sections include a Beautiful section and the Path Along the Ucieza River itself.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The Ucieza River's flow is generally scarce in summer but increases in autumn and winter. Therefore, cycling during autumn or spring might offer a more vibrant riverine landscape. Summer can be pleasant, but the river might be lower. Always check local weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Riverbank of the Ucieza cycling route?
faqs.answer: As the route starts and ends in Villalcázar de Sirga, you can typically find parking within the town. Look for designated parking areas or street parking suitable for visitors accessing the Camino de Santiago, as the town is a historic stop on that pilgrimage route.

faqs.question: Is the Riverbank of the Ucieza cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for dogs on this exact route are not detailed, generally, dogs are permitted on trails in Spain, especially if kept on a leash. Please ensure your dog is well-behaved and that you clean up after them. Always carry water for your pet, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this cycling route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle the Riverbank of the Ucieza circular route. It is a public trail accessible to all.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with sections of other notable paths. You will find it overlaps with parts of the Camiño Francés (Rutas alternativas), the Camino Francés - 04 Burgos a León, and the Camino del Río.

faqs.question: Is the Riverbank of the Ucieza route a loop?
faqs.answer: Yes, this is a circular route, meaning it starts and ends in the same location, Villalcázar de Sirga, without requiring you to retrace your steps.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route is approximately 20.3 kilometers (12.6 miles) long and typically takes around 1 hour and 42 minutes to complete by touring bicycle, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as Villalcázar de Sirga is a historic town and a stop on the Camino de Santiago, you will find amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options available within the town before or after your ride.
